reasoning_model_1: The model accurately generates the content of the text without errors. However, regarding the core requirement of 'gradually raising the pitch to a very high pitch', the model fails completely. The audio maintains a consistent, relatively low and monotone pitch throughout the entire utterance (0:00-0:10). There is no noticeable increase in pitch, let alone a transition to a 'very high' pitch. Additionally, the voice quality is somewhat robotic and has a slight echo, lacking naturalness.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately generates the content. In terms of the core pitch requirement, the model performs partially correctly. The audio starts with a medium pitch and neutral tone (0:00-0:04). Upon reaching 'Wait, I just had a brilliant idea' (0:05), the pitch and energy level noticeably increase to convey excitement. The final segment 'pair it with truffle sauce' (0:08-0:10) maintains this higher energy and pitch, although it follows natural prosodic contours (rising and falling within the phrase) rather than a strictly linear mechanical rise to a squeaky 'very high' pitch. While it doesn't reach an extreme falsetto, the trend of increasing pitch and excitement is clearly present and effectively conveys the semantic shift.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 completely ignores the paralinguistic instruction, delivering a monotone and lower-quality audio file. Model 2 produces high-quality, natural-sounding speech and successfully incorporates a shift in pitch and energy that aligns with the instruction's direction (raising pitch with excitement), even if it doesn't reach an exaggerated 'very high' frequency. Model 2's interpretation is natural and expressive, whereas Model 1 is flat.
